note: Outdated FSA carryover figure. States '$640 carryover' (the 2024 amount); the 2025 carryover is $660, as correctly shown in card 513. The deck otherwise uses 2025 figures, so this is inconsistent/factually outdated.
b: HSA: requires HDHP; employee + employer funded; funds roll over; portable; triple tax benefit. FSA: employer plan; use-it-or-lose-it (grace period or $660 carryover option); not portable. HRA: employer-funded only; not portable but rollover allowed by employer.

note: Outdated FSA carryover figure. States 'up to $640 carryover'; the 2025 carryover limit is $660 (consistent with card 513). 2024 vs 2025 amount.
b: Funded pre-tax. Use-it-or-lose-it rule: must use funds or forfeit. Employers may offer a grace period (2.5 months) OR allow up to $660 carryover (2025) (not both). Eligible for medical AND dependent care expenses.

note: Possible term/definition mismatch. The back ('Fear that the supply of money will stop') describes 'financial dependence,' but the term 'Affluenza' is more commonly defined as dysfunction stemming from excessive/over-affluence (e.g., guilt, lack of motivation, boredom from too much money). The front conflates the two. Recommend verifying against the source curriculum's definition; no confident rewrite provided since the deck appears to treat them as synonyms intentionally.
